If there be a glossy ant-hill covered with holy grass and diirva
grass to the south of a Tilaka tree, there would be water at a depth
of 25 cubits at a distance of 5 cubits to the west of the tree. The
water-vein there would be an easterly one.

If there be an ant-hill to the west of a Kadamba tree {Nauclea
orientalis), there would be a northerly vein carrying inexhaustible
water with the smell of iron at a depth of 2S~ cubits at a distance of
3 cubits to the south of the tree. The signs are : a golden frog at
a depth of 5 cubits; and then, yellow clay.
